The parabolic mirror equation with vertex at origin and focus at 0 4 is x squared equals 16y. Light rays striking point x y reflect to focus following law of reflection where angle of incidence equals angle of reflection. The path derivation involves normal vector at point x y and geometric proof that incoming parallel rays converge at focus. For the galaxy 10 billion light years away the brightness increase comes from 20 percent more light collected. Initial magnitude is 25 and new luminosity ratio is 1.2. Using formula m1 minus m2 equals negative 2.5 log base 10 of 1.2 we find magnitude change. Log base 10 of 1.2 is approximately 0.07918. Multiply by negative 2.5 gives negative 0.19795. New magnitude is 25 minus 0.19795 which equals 24.80205. The brightness increase corresponds to magnitude drop of roughly 0.2. This small shift allows fainter details to be detected in distant galaxies. The new coating enhances data quality significantly for deep space observation. d d d d d d d d d d d d d
